entries.source: "Ask me questions before explaining too much."
entries.value: "Priecaties man jautājumus, pirms es pārāk daudz izskaidrošu"."

entries.source: "Check my answer before giving yours."
"Compare India and Japan"
"Explore the Himalayas"
"Give me a harder version after I try."
"Keep it beginner friendly."
"Use examples from my exam, project, or daily life."
entries.value: "Izmantojiet piemērus no manas eksāmena, projekta vai ikdienas dzīves"."

entries.source: "Why are cities near rivers?"
AI prompts
Ask for a tiny first task.
End with a recap you write yourself.
For Geography Explorer, a useful practice loop is:
entries.value: Geography Explorer izmantošanai ir noderīgs prakses sloksnis:

entries.source: For this mode, the job is simple: Explore countries, cities, rivers, climates, migration, resources, and how geography shapes life.
entries.value: Šajā režīmā uzdevums ir vienkāršs: izpētīt valstis, pilsētās, upes, klimatu, migrāciju, resursi un to, kā ģeogrāfiskā stāvoklī veido dzīvi.

entries.source: Geography Explorer prompts and study loop
entries.value: Geography Explorer uzvedumi un pētījuma slīpums

entries.source: Good prompts usually include three things: the subject, your current level, and the kind of help you want. You do not need perfect wording. You only need enough context for the session to begin.
entries.value: Labi ieteikumi parasti ietver trīs lietas: priekšmetu, jūsu pašreizējo līmeni un to, kāda veida palīdzība jums ir nepieciešama.

entries.source: Humanities

If the response feels too broad, narrow it. Ask for one example, one misconception, one check question, or one next step. If it feels too easy, ask for a challenge. If it feels too hard, ask for a bridge from what you already know.
entries.value: Ja atbilde šķiet pārāk plaša, tad sajūtu. Pieprastu vienu piemēru, vienu nepareizu izpratni, vienu pārbaudījumu jautājumu vai vēl vienu.

entries.source: Make the output more useful
Prompt ideas and a repeatable study loop for getting useful learning results from Geography Explorer on inspir.
entries.value: Īss idejas un atkārtojams pētījuma apdares cikls, lai iegūtu noderīgus mācību rezultātus no Geography Explorer inspir.

entries.source: Reading an AI response is not the same as learning. After the first answer, ask the mode to make you do something with the idea.
entries.value: Ja esat lasis MI atbildi, tas nav tas pats kā mācīšanās.

entries.source: Related learning paths
Request feedback on the part that felt uncertain.
entries.value: Lūdzu, iesakām atbildes uz to, kas man šķita neskaidra.

entries.source: Respond before asking for the solution.
Start with one clear request
State the goal in one sentence.
study skills
That final recap is important. When you explain the idea back, you reveal what is solid and what is still borrowed from the AI.
entries.value: Kad jūs izskaidrojat ideju atpakaļ, jūs atklājat, kas ir solids un kas vēl ir aizņemts no MI.

entries.source: The best learning sessions are adjustable. This mode is tuned for geography explorer with its own prompts, examples, and learning flow.
The fastest way to get value from Geography Explorer is to give the AI a learning job, not just a topic. A topic says what you are interested in. A learning job says what kind of help you need.
entries.value: Labākais veids, kā iegūt vērtību no Geography Explorer, ir sniegt MI mācības darbu, nevis tikai tēmu.

entries.source: Then add a constraint that makes the session more personal:
entries.value: Tad pievieno ierobežojumu, kas padarīs sesiju personīgāku:

entries.source: Try one of these starters:
Turn the mode into practice

Use Geography Explorer when this is the right mode for the job. If you want a related path, try Ethics Dilemmas. You can also browse the AI learning blog for study methods, Socratic learning, flashcards, roleplay, and active recall.
entries.value: Ja vēlaties saistītu ceļu, mēģiniet etiku dilemmas. Jūs varat arī pārlūkot MI mācību blogu par studiju metodēm, Sokrates mācībām, kartīšu komplektuēm, roleplay un aktīvu atceršanu.
